title: The One With Rachels New Dress

jokes.text: It is true what they say: Pregnant bellies look like a drum.
jokes.setup: Phoebe makes an observation about pregnant bellies
jokes.punchline: The comparison to a drum sets up the physical gag that follows
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe logic - takes a random observation and treats it as universal wisdom. Sets up the physical gag nicely.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Your belly is like a drum. I can feel the baby kicking in there.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It's like bongo drums. Do do do do do!

jokes.text: I'm so pregnant that my guitar doesn't fit anymore. So I'm going to play my songs on this drum.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's pregnancy prevents normal guitar playing
jokes.punchline: Her solution is to use her belly as a drum
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag that's perfectly Phoebe - turns a pregnancy inconvenience into a creative musical solution. The absurdity is grounded in character logic.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't play guitar anymore because of my belly. So I'm just gonna use it as a drum instead.

jokes.text: Smelly cat, smelly cat / What are they feeding you? (played on belly drum)
jokes.setup: Phoebe performs her signature song in the new format
jokes.punchline: The familiar song sounds different played on a pregnant belly
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Phoebe's signature song with a fresh twist. The visual of her drumming on her belly while singing is inherently funny.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Smelly cat, smelly cat, what are they feeding you?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Smelly cat, smelly cat, it's not your fault.

jokes.text: That sounds great! I know! And I've only been playing for an hour.
jokes.setup: Monica politely compliments the belly drumming
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's proud response about her rapid mastery of belly percussion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Phoebe's earnest pride in her 'skill' while Monica tries to be supportive. The absurdity of being proud of belly drumming.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's actually really impressive.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Thanks! I've only been doing it for like twenty minutes and I'm already really good at it.

jokes.text: Don't tell me you want to keep more stuff in my uterus.
jokes.setup: Frank Jr. Jr. arrives to ask a favor
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt assumption about what he wants
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe directness - she cuts straight to the uncomfortable truth. The phrasing is both crude and endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Frank Jr. Jr.
dialogue.line: Phoebe, I need to ask you a favor.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! You want me to be a surrogate again!

jokes.text: Frank Jr., Jr. / Wouldn't that be Frank III? / Don't get me started.
jokes.setup: Frank wants to name a baby Frank Jr. Jr.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe points out the logical flaw, Frank dismisses it
jokes.explanation: Good logical humor - Phoebe catches the naming inconsistency. Frank's 'don't get me started' suggests this is an ongoing source of confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. If you're Frank Senior, and he's Frank Junior, wouldn't he be Frank... the Third?

dialogue.speaker: Frank Jr.
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I'm the Junior.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Right, but you're not Senior, so...

dialogue.speaker: Frank Jr.
dialogue.line: I'll be Senior by then.

jokes.text: That's so nice! Cougar.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment about being honored to name the baby
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately suggests 'Cougar' as a name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - genuine emotion followed by completely inappropriate suggestion. The name 'Cougar' is absurd for a baby but delivered with complete sincerity.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm so honored you want me to name the baby!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I have the perfect name: Cougar.

jokes.text: Mommy can have a wife. Daddy can have a bra.
jokes.setup: Question about whether Ben will be confused by a bra
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact explanation of modern family dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good economy - Ross matter-of-factly explains his complex family situation. The parallel structure makes it sound perfectly reasonable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, he'll figure it out. I mean, he sees Carol and Susan together all the time. He knows that Carol is his mother and Susan is her... partner.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So when he sees a bra, he's not going to think it's some big mystery. He'll just think, 'Oh, that's Carol's bra. Or Susan's bra.'

jokes.text: What can we do in 17 minutes... twice? / Well, that's ambitious.
jokes.setup: Ross suggests they have time for intimate activity twice
jokes.punchline: Emily's dry response about his optimism
jokes.explanation: Classic sexual innuendo setup with a good deadpan punchline. Emily's 'ambitious' is perfectly understated.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We still have time for a little... you know.

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: Ross, that's very optimistic. For you.

jokes.text: You can ignore that. That's your son. Believe me, when he's older, he'll understand.
jokes.setup: Ben interrupts the intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Emily's practical approach to the awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor about parenting awkwardness. Emily's matter-of-fact response is mildly amusing but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: Ben! What are you doing up?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I had a bad dream.

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: Well, you can sleep in our bed tonight.

jokes.text: Look at you two bonding. Making us late for the airport. / It's just us getting along is difficult for him... because he doesn't like me. / Come on. That's... / It's true.
jokes.setup: Ross comments on Emily and Susan bonding
jokes.punchline: Susan bluntly states Ross doesn't like her, which he can't deny
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Susan's directness about Ross's feelings puts everyone in an uncomfortable position. Ross can't even deny it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Emily and Susan really hit it off.

dialogue.speaker: Susan
dialogue.line: Well, let's be honest, Ross. You don't like me.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not true.

dialogue.speaker: Susan
dialogue.line: Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I don't like you.

jokes.text: You know? Like... Exxon! / It certainly worked for that Valdez kid.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggests 'Exxon' as a strong corporate name
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dark reference to the Exxon Valdez oil spill
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Chandler takes Phoebe's innocent corporate name suggestion and turns it into a reference to an environmental disaster. Perfect economy and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Exxon. That's a strong name for a corporation.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, unless you're an Alaskan fisherman.

jokes.text: You want a strong name? How about The Hulk?
jokes.setup: Discussion about strong baby names
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests a comic book character
jokes.explanation: Very Joey suggestion - takes the 'strong' requirement literally and suggests a superhero. Sets up the running gag of The Hulk references.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? I got a great name for a baby boy: Hercules.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: All the kids in school would be like, 'Hey, Hercules!' and then they'd be like, 'Yeah?'

jokes.text: I'm not sure about Hulk, but I like a name starting with 'The'.
jokes.setup: Joey's suggestion of 'The Hulk'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe focuses on the wrong part - the article 'The'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - she misses the point entirely and focuses on the least important part. The idea of naming a baby with 'The' is absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You could be The Hulk!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: The Hulk? I don't know... I'm not really a 'The' kind of girl.

jokes.text: You want a good name, go with Joey. Joey's your pal, your buddy. Where is everybody? They're hanging out with Joey!
jokes.setup: Joey making his case for his own name
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic third-person pitch for himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey self-promotion. The third-person enthusiasm and the scenario he creates is endearingly narcissistic but in character.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm gonna go out there and sell myself. I'm a good-looking guy, I'm a talented actor, and I'm a genuinely nice person.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How could they not want me? Joey Tribbiani - he's not just an actor, he's a force of nature! He lights up every room he walks into. Women want him, men want to BE him!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And the best part? He works cheap!

jokes.text: Chandler will be there too. He might be a little late... but he'll be there. And he'll bring you cold soda... if what you need him for is that you're really hot.
jokes.setup: Chandler making his own case for his name
jokes.punchline: His overly specific and somewhat pathetic scenario
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Chandler's self-deprecating pitch acknowledges his flaws while trying to find positives. The cold soda detail is oddly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more... Chandler? I mean, what if I went by a different name? Like, what if I was 'Brad'? Brad Bing. No, that doesn't work. What about 'Steve'? Steve Bing. See, the thing is, I've tried to distance myself from the name Chandler my whole life, but it's like this weird, awkward thing that just... it's me. I'm Chandler Bing. That IS my name. That's who I am. And I can't escape it, even though it sounds like something from a Dickens novel written by someone who had a stroke.

jokes.text: All right, I don't... Maybe I'll just name him The Hulk.
jokes.setup: Joey and Chandler's competing pitches overwhelm Phoebe
jokes.punchline: She returns to the absurd superhero suggestion
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Phoebe's frustration with the competing pitches. The Hulk starts to seem reasonable by comparison.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know what? Forget all that. I think you should be a superhero whose only power is that he can turn into a big pile of wet leaves.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Phoebe...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, no, no, hear me out! And when he does, all the bad guys just jump in the pile because it's so fun!

jokes.text: I shouldn't have mentioned it. I wanted to name my kid that.
jokes.setup: Phoebe threatens to use 'The Hulk'
jokes.punchline: Joey reveals he seriously considered this name for his future child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - he's genuinely protective of 'The Hulk' as a baby name, treating it like a serious option. The sincerity makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm gonna use The Hulk on you!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no! I was seriously considering that for my kid!

jokes.text: It creeps me out choosing other people's sex clothes.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks Monica to choose between outfits for her date
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt assessment of what she's being asked to do
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical discomfort with the situation. The phrase 'sex clothes' is blunt and awkward in a realistic way.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So which outfit do you think is better for my date?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, you want me to choose? That's like asking me to pick a favorite child. Except your children are both ugly.

jokes.text: Then you realized if you bitched, then you would stop cooking... and you would have to make your famous baked potato and Diet Coke.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining Rachel's cooking decision process
jokes.punchline: Rachel's 'famous' meal is just a baked potato and Diet Coke
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Monica knows Rachel's limitations and the pathetic 'famous' meal reveals Rachel's lack of cooking skills.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel's idea of cooking is putting a potato in the microwave and calling it a meal.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's not just a potato, it's a *baked* potato. And I pair it with a Diet Coke for balance.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not balance, that's a cry for help.

jokes.text: I really get crabby when I cook.
jokes.setup: Monica's explanation of Rachel's cooking behavior
jokes.punchline: Rachel's matter-of-fact admission of her attitude problem
jokes.explanation: Simple character admission. Rachel's self-awareness about her cooking personality is mildly amusing but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know, I think the problem is that Rachel doesn't really take cooking seriously.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's not true! I take it very seriously. I'm just not very good at it.

jokes.text: Turns out that Emily is just crazy about Susan. They're going to the theater together. They're going to dinner. They're going horseback riding! God, Susan is so fun!
jokes.setup: Ross reporting on Emily and Susan's activities
jokes.punchline: His increasingly panicked listing of their bonding activities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross's growing panic. The list builds nicely and his final 'Susan is so fun!' has the right jealous undertone.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: They're doing everything together. They went to a museum, they went shopping, they went to lunch...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: They're talking about interior design! Emily's showing her fabric swatches!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Susan is laughing at her jokes. They're exchanging phone numbers!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh God, they're bonding. They're actually bonding!

jokes.text: My friend Susan is so smart. My friend Susan is so funny. My friend Susan is so great.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his paranoia about Susan
jokes.punchline: His repetitive mimicking of Carol's past praise
jokes.explanation: Good use of repetition to show Ross's trauma. The mimicking of Carol's voice (implied) adds to the pathetic quality.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She's always saying things like, 'Oh, Susan's so smart, Susan's so funny, Susan's so talented.' And I'm thinking, 'Yeah, well, Susan's not here anymore, Carol. Susan's gone!' But does she listen? No! It's 'Susan this' and 'Susan that.' I can't compete with Susan! Nobody can compete with Susan!

jokes.text: Two women... stretching! They take a steam, things get playful. Didn't you see 'Personal Best'?
jokes.setup: Ross imagining Emily and Susan at the gym
jokes.punchline: His increasingly explicit imagination and movie reference
jokes.explanation: Ross's paranoid imagination runs wild. The movie reference dates it but shows his specific fears. Moderately funny escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Emily and Susan at the gym together... they're on the StairMaster next to each other, and they're talking about me.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And then Emily says, 'You know, Susan, Ross is really—' and Susan interrupts her and says, 'I know, he's very—'

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And then they're both on the StairMaster and they're both looking at me, and they're both—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's like that scene in Jaws, you know? When all the women realize there's a shark in the water? That's what this is like. Except I'm the shark.

jokes.text: No, but I'm gonna!
jokes.setup: Ross references 'Personal Best' movie
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiastic interest in seeing it
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey - immediately interested in anything that might involve attractive women. Short and in character but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Personal Best? Dude, I gotta see that!

jokes.text: Yeah, definitely. I don't like the name Ross.
jokes.setup: Ross is having relationship anxiety
jokes.punchline: Phoebe randomly critiques his name for the baby
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Phoebe seems to be responding to Ross's crisis but is actually just continuing the baby name conversation. Perfect timing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I just want to make sure we pick the right name for the baby.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I have some thoughts on that. 'Ross' is a terrible name for a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? That's my name!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Exactly. It sounds like a name someone made up when they ran out of real names. Like, 'We need a name... Ross! Done.'

jokes.text: What a weird way to kick me when I'm down!
jokes.setup: Phoebe rejects his name during his emotional crisis
jokes.punchline: Ross's recognition of the terrible timing
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Phoebe's misdirection. Ross's hurt confusion about the timing is relatable and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm having an emotional crisis and you're upset about my name?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Your name is Ross!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know my name is Ross.

jokes.text: Something like this would never happen to The Hulk, you know.
jokes.setup: Ross's relationship troubles
jokes.punchline: He enviously compares himself to the superhero name suggestion
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Ross's desperation - he's now envying a comic book character's relationship prospects. Absurd but emotionally grounded.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Why does everyone else get to be happy? Monica has Chandler, Rachel has... well, she's working on it. Even Phoebe found someone. And me? I'm stuck being... being...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What, like a superhero? 'Captain Dateless'?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, exactly. Except Captain Dateless actually has a cool name.

jokes.text: You know... Never mind. My girlfriend's a lesbian.
jokes.setup: Ross starts to defend with comic book knowledge
jokes.punchline: He gives up and returns to his real problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ross realizes arguing about comic books won't help his real crisis. The defeated delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Actually, if you look at the comic book canon, Spider-Man's web fluid has a tensile strength of—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know what? Forget it. I can't believe Rachel is dating Paolo.

jokes.text: Joey Buttafucco. Yeah, that guy really hurt us.
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions there are no famous Joeys
jokes.punchline: Joey acknowledges how the scandal damaged his name's reputation
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference. Joey's personal sense of injury over sharing a name with a scandal figure is endearing and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: There are no famous Joeys.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, there was Joey Buttafuoco.

jokes.text: How about a compromise then? What if it's... Chan-no-ey?
jokes.setup: Joey and Chandler can't agree on names
jokes.punchline: Phoebe creates a bizarre hybrid name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sounds like a real compromise but is actually absurd. The pronunciation makes it funnier than just seeing it written.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What about Chandler Joey? Or Joandler? Pheeandler? Wait, that's me.

jokes.text: Joey? Think about it. He'll never be President. There will never be a President Joey.
jokes.setup: Chandler making his case against Joey
jokes.punchline: The specific argument about presidential prospects
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the name war - Chandler goes for long-term consequences. The presidential argument is both silly and somewhat valid.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more qualified to be president?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, you'd have to actually run for office first.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I've run from things my whole life. How is this different?

jokes.text: Chandler is the stupidest name I've heard in my life! It's not a name! It's barely even a word. It's like chandelier but it's not!
jokes.setup: The name argument reaches a breaking point
jokes.punchline: Joey's passionate attack on Chandler's name and etymology
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Joey gets genuinely heated and his analysis of 'chandelier but it's not' is both stupid and insightful. The passion makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, okay. 'Chandler'? What kind of name is Chandler? It sounds like you should be working in a department store or something. 'Can I help you find some slacks?' No! It's a terrible name!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's not that bad.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Not that bad?! It's worse than bad! You know what your name sounds like? A candle maker. A guy who makes candles!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, that's what it means.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Exactly! Why would your parents name you after a job? That's insane! I mean, at least my name is cool. Joey Tribbiani — that's a name with style, with pizzazz!

jokes.text: You're right. I have a horrible, horrible name.
jokes.setup: Joey's vicious attack on his name
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defeated acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - instead of fighting back, he's crushed by the criticism. The repetition of 'horrible' shows how deeply it affected him.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Could you BE any more pathetic?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I could try.

jokes.text: Behind you. They used to live here, and sometimes they migrate back over.
jokes.setup: Joshua reacts to something behind him
jokes.punchline: Rachel's casual explanation of the birds as if they're seasonal visitors
jokes.explanation: Great visual setup - treating birds in the apartment like they have migratory patterns. Rachel's matter-of-fact explanation makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua
dialogue.line: What the—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, don't worry about them. They come every year around this time.

jokes.text: Farm birds really kind of freak me out!
jokes.setup: Birds are flying around the apartment
jokes.punchline: Joshua's specific phobia revelation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the physical comedy to follow. The specific 'farm birds' distinction is mildly amusing but serves mainly as plot setup.

jokes.text: All gone. So, farm birds, huh? It's my only weird thing, I swear. I would have told you. I didn't know that they'd be here.
jokes.setup: Joshua has successfully dealt with the birds
jokes.punchline: His embarrassed over-explanation of his phobia
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor - Joshua over-explains his phobia like it's a major character flaw. The apologetic tone is endearing but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua
dialogue.line: Look, I know what you're all thinking. But it's not irrational! Birds are unpredictable. They have those beady little eyes, and they move so fast you can't see where they're going. Plus, have you ever really looked at their feet? They're like tiny alien claws!

jokes.text: They're across the hall. That's two doors away. It'll take them a long time to peck their way back over here.
jokes.setup: Joshua is still worried about the birds
jokes.punchline: Rachel's logical but absurd explanation of bird travel time
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - Rachel treats the birds like they're planning a coordinated attack. The 'peck their way back' image is amusing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, they're birds, Joshua. They fly. It probably only takes them like, what, twenty minutes?

jokes.text: That's not funny.
jokes.setup: Rachel's joke about the birds pecking their way back
jokes.punchline: Joshua's flat rejection of her attempt at humor
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Joshua's serious response to Rachel's joke creates awkwardness. The economy of the line makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: The birds will just peck their way back out.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua
dialogue.line: That's not funny.

jokes.text: They can smell the fear.
jokes.setup: Joshua's obvious anxiety about the birds
jokes.punchline: Rachel's nature documentary-style observation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to animal behavior tropes. Short and punchy, treating the domestic birds like wild predators.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He's like a nature documentary. 'And here we see the male bird in his natural habitat, nervously preening his feathers, unaware that the female is about to reject him.'

jokes.text: I asked if he wanted to eat. He said no. I asked if he wanted to sleep. He said no. I asked what he wanted to do. He said no. So he's sweeping.
jokes.setup: Carol asks about Ben's activities
jokes.punchline: Ross's logical conclusion from Ben's negative responses
jokes.explanation: Good parenting humor - the escalation through Ross's attempts and Ben's consistent 'no' leading to the random activity is relatable and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: So what did you do today, Ben?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Nothing.

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: Did you play with your toys?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: Did you watch TV?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, you must be dead.

jokes.text: Carol, so were we.
jokes.setup: Carol says Susan is in a loving, committed relationship
jokes.punchline: Ross points out their marriage was too
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - Ross makes the obvious point that Carol is forgetting. Perfect economy and devastating logic.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, their marriage was a loving, committed relationship too.

jokes.text: I didn't really believe it until you just said it!
jokes.setup: Ross's paranoid scenario about Susan and Emily
jokes.punchline: Carol's realization that she's convinced herself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Carol goes from dismissive to panicked. Her admission that Ross convinced her is both funny and concerning.

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm starting to think Susan might be right about Emily...

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I've convinced myself.

jokes.text: None of the other kids believed me, but I swear that duck pushed me.
jokes.setup: Scene transition to Rachel and Joshua
jokes.punchline: Rachel's random childhood trauma about a duck
jokes.explanation: Random Rachel moment that connects to the bird theme. The image of a duck deliberately pushing a child is absurd but believable from Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: When I was a kid, a duck chased me. I couldn't have been more than seven. It was the most traumatic thing that ever happened to me.

jokes.text: It would have been, but my parents just moved here. You should know this place is a real babe magnet.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if the fancy house was a babe magnet growing up
jokes.punchline: Joshua clarifies the timeline and turns it back on Rachel
jokes.explanation: Mild flirtation with a logical clarification. Joshua's attempt to turn the compliment around is sweet but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Was the fancy house a babe magnet growing up?

dialogue.speaker: Joshua
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't grow up in that house. My parents bought it after I was in college.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua
dialogue.line: But speaking of babe magnets, how many guys have you gone through since college?

jokes.text: We cut the trip short. France sucks! / Yep. Sucks!
jokes.setup: Joshua's parents return early from Europe
jokes.punchline: Their matter-of-fact dismissal of France and agreement
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation - the parents' casual dismissal of an entire country, with the echo for emphasis. Mildly amusing cultural ignorance.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua's Mother
dialogue.line: Well, we're back from France.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua's Father
dialogue.line: Dreadful place.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua's Mother
dialogue.line: Absolutely dreadful.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I know. I can do more than cook.
jokes.setup: Rachel appears in revealing lingerie
jokes.punchline: Her proud announcement of her other skills
jokes.explanation: Sets up the major visual gag of the episode. Rachel's confidence about her 'skills' is both sexy and awkward given what's about to happen.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can also cook, clean, and do laundry!

jokes.text: I like her. She seems smart.
jokes.setup: Rachel is wearing revealing lingerie
jokes.punchline: The mother's focus on Rachel's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag payoff - the mother's completely inappropriate focus on intelligence while Rachel is in lingerie. Great deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: Joshua, that $500 was for groceries.
jokes.setup: Rachel is in expensive-looking lingerie
jokes.punchline: The father assumes Joshua paid for it with grocery money
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - the father makes a logical but wrong assumption. The grocery money detail adds to the absurdity of the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua's father
dialogue.line: Did my son buy you that outfit?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes, he did.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua's father
dialogue.line: That must have cost a fortune. Where'd he get the money?

dialogue.speaker: Joshua's father
dialogue.line: Please tell me that's not the grocery money.

jokes.text: I work in fashion. And this is a real dress, actually. They're wearing it in Milan. Part of my job is to wear the clothes and see how people respond.
jokes.setup: Rachel needs to explain her revealing outfit
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly elaborate lie about fashion work
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe escalation - Rachel digs herself deeper with an increasingly specific lie. The Milan reference and job description show her panic-induced creativity.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, so I was helping out at this really important photo shoot for a major fashion designer...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And they needed someone to model the clothes, so I volunteered because, you know, it's fashion.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: But then the designer said my regular clothes weren't... fashion-forward enough, so they gave me this outfit to wear instead.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's actually very expensive. It's a limited edition piece that most people can't even pull off.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Plus, I got paid! Well, I mean, I will get paid. Once the check clears. From the fashion people.

jokes.text: Obviously, in this case... I am going to report back: 'U.S.A. Not ready.'
jokes.setup: Rachel's elaborate fashion testing lie
jokes.punchline: Her assessment of America's readiness for the outfit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and self-awareness - Rachel turns her embarrassing situation into a professional assessment. The formal report format makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I think America is ready.

jokes.text: No use wasting this baby just lying around the house.
jokes.setup: Rachel is dressed for seduction
jokes.punchline: The father's practical approach to not wasting the outfit
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the visual gag - the father treats the revealing outfit like any other dress. His practicality makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua's father
dialogue.line: You know, that's a very nice outfit. It would be a shame to waste it.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua's father
dialogue.line: Why don't you go seduce my son?

jokes.text: We'll be eating and, of course, you'll be wearing that.
jokes.setup: They're going to dinner with Rachel in lingerie
jokes.punchline: The mother's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's predicament - now she has to go to dinner in the outfit. The mother's casual acceptance makes it more awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Joshua's Mother
dialogue.line: Well, I can see you're not wearing much under that dress.

jokes.text: Nope, you're right. It is a ridiculous name! / Yes, it is! From now on, I have no first name.
jokes.setup: Joey's earlier attack on Chandler's name
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic decision to abandon his first name entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler overreaction - instead of defending his name, he decides to eliminate it entirely. The dramatic escalation is very in character.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more insulted? That's it. From now on, I'm going by my middle name. I'm Muriel now.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Muriel?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's right. Muriel Bing. Has a nice ring to it, don't you think?

jokes.text: You're just Bing?
jokes.setup: Chandler says he has no first name
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's clarification of what that would mean
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows how ridiculous Chandler's plan is. 'Bing' alone sounds like a sound effect or search engine.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So your name is just Chandler Chandler?

jokes.text: Okay, for now, temporarily, you can call me... Clint.
jokes.setup: Chandler needs a new first name
jokes.punchline: His choice of the overly masculine 'Clint'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character choice - Chandler picks the most masculine name possible to overcompensate. The hesitation before saying it shows his uncertainty.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I need a new first name. Something more masculine.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What about Clint?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Clint? That's perfect! Clint Bing. I'm gonna be a whole new man.

jokes.text: No way are you cool enough to pull off 'Clint'. / What name am I cool enough to pull off? / Gene.
jokes.setup: Joey rejects Chandler's chosen name
jokes.punchline: Joey's assessment that Chandler could only pull off 'Gene'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character assassination - Joey's brutal honesty about Chandler's coolness level. 'Gene' is perfectly mundane and deflating.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No way! You can't pull off 'Chandler.' You gotta go with 'Gene.'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Gene? Why Gene?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Because that's the only name you could pull off.

jokes.text: What's up with Gene?
jokes.setup: Chandler leaves after the name discussion
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately adopts the new name
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Phoebe immediately starts using the name Chandler rejected, showing how quickly she moves on from his protests.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm Chandler now!

jokes.text: I jumped up and my boob popped out. It's all right. I got nice boobs.
jokes.setup: Rachel describes the dinner disaster
jokes.punchline: Her casual self-assessment after the wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Classic Rachel - turning an embarrassing moment into a compliment to herself. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Her casual self-assessment after the wardrobe malfunction

jokes.text: She and Susan are going to a poetry reading together. / So? / Poetry? Susan's gay. They're being gay together!
jokes.setup: Emily and Susan are attending cultural events
jokes.punchline: Ross's paranoid interpretation of poetry as inherently gay
jokes.explanation: Excellent paranoid escalation - Ross connects poetry to sexuality in his panic. The phrase 'being gay together' is both ridiculous and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying poetry is... gay?

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: No, Ross, not all poetry is gay.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: But some of it is, right? Because that's what I'm hearing.

dialogue.speaker: Susan
dialogue.line: Oh my God, no. We just went to see a poem about nature.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Nature? That's very... suspicious.

jokes.text: She turned you into this untrusting, crazy, jealous... sycophant!
jokes.setup: Monica criticizes how Carol changed Ross
jokes.punchline: Her use of 'sycophant' which doesn't quite fit
jokes.explanation: Monica's angry rant with a vocabulary word that doesn't quite work. Sets up Ross's confusion about the word meaning.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She completely changed him. He used to be so funny and now he's just a... a sycophant!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A sycophant?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes! Someone who changes who they are to please someone else.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know what a sycophant is. I just don't think that's the word you're looking for.

jokes.text: So I don't know what 'sycophant' means, but the rest is right.
jokes.setup: Monica calls Ross a sycophant
jokes.punchline: Ross admits ignorance but accepts the other criticisms
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness joke - Ross admits his vocabulary limitations while accepting the harsh assessment. The honesty makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're a sycophant!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't even know what that means.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: But I'm not going to argue with the other two things you said.

jokes.text: So up until '92, '93, he was very trusting. Then '94 hit, Carol left him, and bam! Paranoid City!
jokes.setup: Discussion of Ross's trust issues timeline
jokes.punchline: Monica's analysis with 'Paranoid City' as the destination
jokes.explanation: Good character analysis with a fun phrase. 'Paranoid City' as a psychological destination is creative and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, so Ross has trust issues. And apparently they started when he was seven years old. So for the past... *counts on fingers* ...twenty-eight years, he's been living in Paranoid City. Population: Ross.

jokes.text: This is so fun!
jokes.setup: Everyone is analyzing Ross's psychological damage
jokes.punchline: Rachel's enthusiastic enjoyment of Ross's pain
jokes.explanation: Classic Rachel moment - finding entertainment in someone else's psychological breakdown. The enthusiasm contrasts with Ross's pain.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, this is so much fun!

jokes.text: The '92 Ross wouldn't.
jokes.setup: Advice about not letting Carol ruin his relationship with Emily
jokes.punchline: Monica's reference to the better version of Ross
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that treats past Ross like a different person. The specificity of the year makes it funnier and more poignant.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You can't let Carol ruin your relationship with Emily. You have to be the better version of Ross.

jokes.text: Well, you know what? I hope Emily is a lesbian.
jokes.setup: Ross's frustration with everyone's advice
jokes.punchline: His petulant wish that would validate his paranoia
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ross would rather be right than happy. The petulant reversal shows his frustration with being analyzed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Fine! You know what? I don't want your advice anymore. I don't want anybody's advice. From now on, I'm making all my own decisions. And I hope you're all happy when I'm miserable!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We will be.

jokes.text: Am I a Mark or a John? / You're not tall enough to be a Mark. But you might make a good Barney.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks for help choosing a new name
jokes.punchline: Joey's assessment based on physical attributes and suggests 'Barney'
jokes.explanation: Joey's logical approach to name selection based on height. 'Barney' is even worse than 'Gene' in terms of coolness factor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I need help choosing a new name.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, let's see. You're short, you're stocky, you've got that funny hair... I'd say 'Barney.'

jokes.text: So tomorrow, I'll either be Mark Johnson or John Markson.
jokes.setup: Chandler is serious about legally changing his name
jokes.punchline: His two boring name options that are basically the same
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how desperate Chandler is. The two names are interchangeably boring, which is the point.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm thinking Chad or Brad.

jokes.text: You've got problems because of you, not your name!
jokes.setup: Chandler blames his problems on his name
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt assessment that Chandler is the real problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey honesty - he cuts through Chandler's rationalization with brutal truth. The delivery would be both funny and harsh.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I'm not saying your name doesn't suck, but dude, you ARE a major problem.

jokes.text: This has got to stop! Chandler is a great name. In fact... I'm going to name the baby Chandler.
jokes.setup: Chandler's self-hatred about his name
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic decision to honor him
jokes.explanation: Perfect resolution to the naming arc. Phoebe's dramatic gesture validates Chandler while resolving the baby name plot. Excellent character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Could you BE any more ashamed of your name?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I mean, my parents named me after a city in Massachusetts, a biblical king, and my mother's maiden name. It's like they were trying to ruin my life.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I'm changing my name to Chandler. From now on, I'm Chandler Bing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? Why would you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Because someone has to love your name, and apparently it's not going to be you.

jokes.text: Nice luggage. / I was going to say.
jokes.setup: Emily returns with luggage
jokes.punchline: Ross and Monica's synchronized appreciation
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about sibling synchronization. The shared appreciation is realistic but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Emily!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Emily!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God, hi!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, hi!

jokes.text: No tongue.
jokes.setup: Ross and Emily reunite after his paranoid episode
jokes.punchline: Emily's specific limitation on their welcome kiss
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat - Emily sets boundaries after Ross's behavior. The specificity of 'no tongue' is both funny and telling about their relationship status.

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: Ross, I'm so glad you're okay. But when you kiss me hello, please don't use your tongue.

tmdb_overview: Rachel has an awkward encounter with Joshua's parents. Phoebe is offered the chance to name one of the triplets. Ross becomes paranoid when Emily goes to London with Susan.
insight: Four-minute cold open carries the episode with rapid-fire observational comedy.
